seo.title: Georgia Market Intelligence | Perfect Mission
seo.description: Georgia is a compelling add-on market: open foreign ownership, tourism growth, and tax-light positioning, with more geopolitical edge than EU-adjacent CEE markets.

hero.title: Georgia: open, tax-light, geopolitically sharper
hero.subtitle: A plausible future addition to the market stack for operators who want a more open foreign-ownership regime than Albania.

summary.one_line_verdict: Georgia is easier than Albania on ownership openness and often easier to explain than deeper frontier markets, but it sits in a sharper geopolitical envelope.

market_stage.label: Open-growth / non-EU frontier
market_stage.description: Tourism, transactions, and foreign-buyer openness all support the story, but the non-EU and geopolitical framing changes the risk profile.

summary.operator_angle: Potentially very attractive as a satellite market once the core platform is stable.
summary.investor_angle: Easy to like because ownership is open and the tax profile is light, but geopolitics always enters the conversation.
summary.base_case: Strong candidate for a future added market; not necessary for the first site release if the current map must stay tight.

quick_facts.label: Tourism signal
quick_facts.value: 5.8 million international visitors in 2025, up 7.0% YoY

quick_facts.label: Foreign ownership signal
quick_facts.value: Foreign nationals generally enjoy full real-estate ownership rights

quick_facts.label: Investor-visa signal
quick_facts.value: Property-based residency threshold rose to US$150,000 from March 2026

strategy_fit.why: Tbilisi remains the default high-liquidity city.
Batumi and resort zones are obvious but must be filtered carefully.
A key differentiator versus many other frontier markets.
The market must always be read inside its regional risk envelope.

development_reality.headline: Open ownership helps; geopolitics still matters
development_reality.copy: Georgia is attractive because foreigners can own directly and the tax environment is relatively light. It is less attractive if those benefits cause you to ignore macro and geopolitical risk.
process_notes: Ownership openness is a real edge.
Tourism is supportive but not sufficient on its own.
Georgia is a candidate market, not necessarily a day-one site priority.

foreign_ownership.summary: Georgia is one of the most foreigner-open real-estate markets in the wider region.
foreign_ownership.caution: Direct ownership simplicity does not eliminate macro and geopolitical risk.

priority_cities.thesis: Best for liquidity and year-round demand.
High visibility and strong tourism logic.
Interesting only for niche tourism product.

risks: Residency-rule changes can distort buyer behaviour.

lifestyle_base.copy: A plausible future base or extended-stay market for a cross-border operator who accepts the geopolitical overlay.

cta.headline: Need the verified version?
cta.body: Request a country memo or submit a specific site, listing, plot, or market thesis for verification.
